etymology.description: semantic 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") + phonetic 台(台) (tái) -> beginning; first
etymology.interpretation: The character 始(shǐ, "beginning") combines 女(女) (nǚ, "woman"), suggesting the idea of origin or giving birth, with 台(台) (tái), which provides the sound. This creates the concept of 'the start or beginning,' akin to a woman initiating the cycle of life.

examples.chinese: 这是一段新的开始(這是一段新的開始)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī duàn xīn de kāishǐ.

examples.chinese: 他始终如一地支持我(他始終如一地支持我)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shǐzhōng rúyī de zhīchí wǒ.

examples.chinese: 从始至终(從始至終)，他都很冷静(他都很冷靜)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng shǐ zhì zhōng, tā dōu hěn lěngjìng.
examples.english: From beginning to end, he remained calm.
